overview.tagline: An Android-first, age-only gopass password client.

goals.ageOnly: age-only encryption — no GPG secret encryption, just modern age
goals.androidFirst: Android-first UX, with a matching desktop app from one codebase
goals.gitSync: sync over git to your own repository — no cloud, no third-party servers
goals.gopassCompatible: gopass-compatible secret, template, and preset formats
goals.securityFirst: secrets never reach the WebView; the master key is wiped after every operation

ack.intro: gpm stands on the shoulders of these open-source projects.

gopass.desc: The password manager gpm is conceptually aligned with. gpm mirrors gopass's secret, template, and create-wizard formats, narrowing its scope to an age-only, mobile-first client.

age.desc: The modern age encryption that powers gpm's core. Every secret is age-encrypted; this Rust library (rage) does the heavy lifting.

tauri.desc: The framework that ships one Rust + web codebase to Android and the desktop, with a security model that keeps secrets in Rust and out of the WebView.

vue.desc: The progressive frontend framework behind gpm's reactive, accessible UI.

git2.desc: Git operations — clone, pull, push, commit — over HTTPS and SSH, the backbone of gpm's repository sync.

rpgp.desc: Pure-Rust OpenPGP used to verify GPG-signed commits, with no OpenSSL or C dependencies.

nucleo.desc: The fuzzy matcher behind gpm's fast entry search.

licenses.degradedNotice: This inventory is incomplete — some license text is missing. Rebuilding with the full toolchain regenerates it.
licenses.noLicenseText: License text unavailable for this package.
